topic: Lunar 18.6-yr cycle via gears — change-driven verification of c4 patch landings
change_summary: detail_verdict_length grew from 1741 to 2120 chars (+21.8%); sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-24T05:42:10Z; obe_skipped=4 (WIN-019/013/009/067 already re-reviewed since audit). The growth corresponds to the integration of three c4 holes_found recommendations into the detail_verdict_text: (a) c4 hole #1 paragraph-bridge sentence ('A defender may reply that any young framework deserves time…'), (b) c4 hole #2 LLR phrasing rewrite ('Lunar laser ranging constrains the gravitational-torque orbit model to millimeter-level range precision, yielding nodal-period consistency to many decimal places'), and (c) c4 hole #4 ±5° gloss ('the lunar-standstill amplitude: major vs minor standstills at ~28.6°/~18.3°'). Verdict unchanged. The first appearance of all three additions in the git tree is commit 27b8c5b (workspace-sync 2026-05-24T23:07:14Z), implying the edits were made in the FUSE workspace and rescued by workspace-sync; no decider applied-patches/ entry references the bridge sentence or LLR rewrite.
our_argument_summary: 'Std Model Explains' — gravitational torque (Clairaut 1749 + standard perturbation theory) produces the 18.613-yr nodal regression; the dome's '(epicyclic gears, no Newton)' label supplies no driver, no gear tooth counts, no rate, and no amplitude. The c4→c5 deltas land three of the five c4 holes (bridge sentence, LLR phrasing, ±5° gloss) and leave two open: c4 hole #3 (absolute 'no testable deviation at any precision at all' phrasing still present, recommendation to soften with 'as stated' qualifier not applied) and c4 hole #5 (Saros/nodal conflation in detail_evidence — the 'Antikythera mechanism (~100 BC) and appears in Babylonian eclipse records predating that' string is unchanged). The c4-flagged ISS-1196 carry-forward was closed by decider-verify-already-done on 2026-04-20 but the underlying text was not patched at that time and remains unpatched at c5 — a tracking-discipline failure worth re-surfacing. The CONFIRMED-anchoring template has not propagated to the cross-WIN cluster (WIN-021, WIN-022, WIN-037) that c4 flagged in stronger_arguments.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt — the single metadata line: 'WIN-020 — Lunar Declination 18.6-Year Cycle CONFIRMED (epicyclic gears, no Newton)'

raw-text/03-predictions.txt, 04-coordinates.txt, 05-model.txt, 06-evolution.txt, 07-ai-context.txt, 08-live-power.txt — confirmed no WIN-020 / 18.6-yr nodal content via grep (the W020 hit in 03-predictions.txt is a different namespace: 'W020: Roaring 40s 500hPa anomaly')
raw-text/monitor.py — not present in this raw-text dump; Cycles 1–4 all established no handler exists for WIN-020. This c5 cycle does not change that finding.
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No calculation pipeline. WIN-020 is metadata only: one line in 02-wins.txt that asserts CONFIRMED status and labels the mechanism '(epicyclic gears, no Newton)'. No gear tooth counts, no driver, no rate derivation, no amplitude prediction, no monitor.py handler.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Neither. No pred==obs check exists for WIN-020 — not hardcoded, not computed, not live-fetched.
data_sources_used: None — no data is fetched or compared for WIN-020
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None — no computation exists, so no tolerance / precondition logic either.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: The detail_verdict_text correctly states 'no monitor.py handler, no hardcoded expected value, and no live fetch; the period appears only as a metadata label.' This is consistent with code_analysis.monitoring='none' and with the raw-text grep result. No code-claim discrepancies introduced by the c4→c5 edits.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Re-confirmed at c5: no monitor.py handler exists for WIN-020. 02-wins.txt carries a single metadata line ('WIN-020 — Lunar Declination 18.6-Year Cycle CONFIRMED (epicyclic gears, no Newton)'); no other raw-text file references WIN-020, the 18.613-year nodal regression, or any handler. Per tag definition ('no domain, no data fetch, no comparison logic'), 'none' is correct.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The 18.613-year nodal regression is derived from solar gravitational torque on the Moon's inclined orbit (Clairaut 1749–52; standard perturbation theory; lunar laser ranging confirmation since the 1970s). The dome preserves the same numerical value and renames the mechanism 'epicyclic gears' with no independent derivation — a textbook relabel.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The period has been measured / encoded for over two millennia (Antikythera mechanism ~100 BC tracks the *Saros* eclipse cycle — 18.030 yr, not the 18.613-yr nodal cycle; the nodal regression itself was derived from first principles by Clairaut in 1749; modern textbooks). The dome adopted the long-known value and labeled it CONFIRMED — the archetypal Part 3.1b trivial-transcription failure mode.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation from disc_radius (20,015 km), firmament_height (9,086 km), sun_altitude (5,733 km), moon_altitude (2,534 km), or any other dome-specific parameter exists anywhere in the dome content. 'Epicyclic gears' is asserted as a descriptor; no gear ratio, drive, or rate is computed from dome geometry.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward from c4 hole #5 (originally c2/c3): detail_evidence still conflates the Saros (~18.03-yr) and nodal (~18.613-yr) cycles. The string 'encoded in the Antikythera mechanism (~100 BC) and appears in Babylonian eclipse records predating that' is unchanged at c5. The Antikythera mechanism's eclipse dial tracks the Saros (223 synodic months = 6585.32 days = 18.030 Julian years; see Freeth et al. 2006), NOT the nodal regression (6798.383 days / 365.25 = 18.613 yr; mathematically a distinct cycle). Saros and nodal differ by ~0.58 yr — a difference exceeding the implied precision of 'over 2,000 years of observation'. Compounding this: ISS-1196 was closed as 'fixed by decider-verify-already-done' on 2026-04-20T20:37:50Z, but the detail_evidence text it was closed against was never actually patched — the close is a tracking-discipline false-positive. Because the post-hoc critique invokes 'over 2,000 years of observation' to argue the value is publicly known, the imprecision matters: a defender can correctly note that ancient civilizations did NOT measure the 18.613-yr nodal regression — they tracked the Saros for eclipse prediction. Standard physics (Clairaut 1749) derived the 18.613-yr value from first principles ~275 years ago, which is still more than enough to defeat any 'novel prediction' claim, but the prose should be accurate.
holes_found.recommendation: Open a patch against detail_evidence: replace 'is encoded in the Antikythera mechanism (~100 BC) and appears in Babylonian eclipse records predating that' with '(Antikythera tracked the closely related ~18.03-yr Saros via its eclipse dial; the 18.613-yr nodal period was derived from first principles by Clairaut 1749 and confirmed observationally over the centuries since)'. Also re-open ISS-1196 (or open a successor ISS) — the verify-already-done close was a false positive; the conflation is still in detail_evidence at c5.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward from c4 hole #3: 'the dome's gear metaphor produces no rate, no amplitude, and no testable deviation at any precision at all' is still in the detail_verdict_text exactly as flagged at c4. The recommended softening ('...and, as stated, no testable deviation at any precision' or '...supplies no deliverable that could be falsified at any precision') was not applied. The absolute 'at any precision at all' phrasing is rhetorically vulnerable to the defender retort 'IF the dome supplied tooth counts, deviations WOULD be testable; the claim that gears can never produce a testable deviation is a strawman of the gear metaphor itself.' The kernel-of-truth block already concedes Antikythera is the existence-proof for testable astronomical gearing, so the prose contradicts itself if read absolutely.
holes_found.recommendation: Apply the c4 hole #3 recommendation: insert 'as stated' before 'no testable deviation' — '...the dome's gear metaphor produces no rate, no amplitude, and, as stated, no testable deviation at any precision' — or alternatively rewrite the clause to '...supplies no deliverable that could be falsified at any precision.' This is a 5-character edit and closes the c4-flagged minor hole without changing the verdict.

holes_found.description: New hole introduced by the c4 hole #1 patch: the bridge-sentence integration creates a double-strong redundancy in paragraph 3. The two adjacent <strong>-wrapped sentences both assert the same proposition: '...rebuttal is pre-empted on the dome author's own terms.' immediately followed by '...This verdict is returned against the dome author's own stated bar, not a maturity standard we have chosen.' Both clauses bold the 'author's own bar' framing; rendering this in the HTML produces two consecutive bold sentences that say almost the same thing. The c4 hole #1 recommendation gave two alternative fixes (insert the bridge OR replace 'This verdict' with 'This critique'), and the EXP-186 integration applied the first option but did not also relax the second clause from 'verdict' to 'critique'. The two are now slightly redundant. Severity is minor because the meaning is correct and a careful reader will parse the second sentence as a clarification of the first, but the doubled bolding draws attention to the redundancy.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) drop the ... wrappers from one of the two sentences to de-emphasize the redundancy, OR (b) replace 'This verdict is returned against...' with 'The verdict is returned against...' to soften the demonstrative repetition, OR (c) merge them into a single sentence: 'A defender may reply that any young framework deserves time to derive numerical values — mature theories earn their precision over centuries — but this verdict is returned against the dome author's own stated bar, not a maturity standard we have chosen.' Option (c) is the cleanest.

holes_found.description: detail_evidence still asserts 'Modern lunar laser ranging (LLR) confirms lunar orbital parameters to millimeter precision.' This is the same imprecise phrasing that c4 hole #2 surgically corrected in the detail_verdict_text. The fix did not propagate to the detail_evidence field — readers who skim the evidence block (which most do, since it sits above the verdict) still encounter the loose conflation between 'mm range precision' (the LLR range observable) and 'parameter precision' (a derived quantity). Severity is minor because the underlying physics is well-established, but the symmetric correction (range-precision is a range claim, not a parameter claim) should propagate.
holes_found.recommendation: Apply a parallel detail_evidence patch: replace 'Modern lunar laser ranging (LLR) confirms lunar orbital parameters to millimeter precision' with 'Modern lunar laser ranging (LLR) constrains lunar orbital parameters to millimeter-level range precision, yielding orbital constants accurate to many decimal places.' The c4 hole #2 rewrite for detail_verdict_text used the same construction; mirroring it in detail_evidence aligns the two fields and removes the residual hand-wave.

holes_found.description: Mixed quote-character usage in detail_verdict_text. Within paragraph 3, the bridge sentence uses curly apostrophe ('s) — 'dome author's own terms', 'A younger-model carve-out' — while the surrounding paragraphs use straight apostrophe ('s) — 'dome author's own definition', 'monitor.py', 'live fetch'. The Unicode mix renders fine but it's a stylistic inconsistency a careful reader (or a search-and-replace pass) will flag. The mix arose because the bridge sentence text was hand-typed (or copied from a context that used curly quotes) while the c2/c3-era surrounding prose used straight ASCII apostrophes.
holes_found.recommendation: Normalize all apostrophes in detail_verdict_text to straight ASCII ' (or to curly, but pick one). The other WIN-020 fields use straight ASCII throughout; converting paragraph 3 to match is the lower-friction option. This is a cosmetic patch that decider can do in a single search-and-replace.

unresolved_prior_cycle.note: ISS-1196 was closed by 'decider-verify-already-done' on 2026-04-20T20:37:50Z but the Saros/nodal conflation in detail_evidence was NOT actually patched at that time; the offending string survives unchanged at c5. Decider should EITHER re-open ISS-1196 OR open a successor ISS pointing at this c5 review.

stronger_arguments.description: Cross-WIN template propagation gap (carry-forward from c4 stronger_arguments). The CONFIRMED-anchoring + maturity-bar preemption combo that c4 integrated for WIN-020 has NOT propagated to WIN-021 (gyroscopic precession rate), WIN-022 (1990 magnetic phase transition), or WIN-037 (>=28 nT field decay). WIN-021's detail_verdict_text uses the trivial-transcription paragraph but skips the CONFIRMED-anchoring framing. WIN-022 uses a 'field-specific published dataset' variant. WIN-037 uses the prospective OpenTimestamps consideration. The c4 'stronger_arguments' suggestion was to propagate the CONFIRMED-anchoring template across these cousins; a single decider patch pass could harmonize them. This isn't a c5 blocker for WIN-020 — it's an open invitation that's still open.
sources: data/wins.json — WIN-021, WIN-022, WIN-037 detail_verdict_text fields (c5-clone state)

stronger_arguments.description: Quantitative grip the c5 detail_verdict_text alludes to but still doesn't state: gravitational-torque theory predicts a nodal regression rate of 19.341 °/yr (= 360° / 18.613 yr), and modern LLR-tied ephemerides (DE440/DE441) propagate the lunar node with sub-milliarcsecond residuals over multi-decade fits. The dome's 'epicyclic gears' produces NO rate prediction — not a less-precise one, but literally zero. Stating the asymmetry as '19.34 °/yr (LLR-confirmed standard model) vs. 0 °/yr (dome gear metaphor)' would convert 'no rate' from a qualitative complaint to a quantitative one. c4 raised this in stronger_arguments[1]; it remains pending.
sources: Park, R. S. et al. (2021) 'The JPL Planetary and Lunar Ephemerides DE440 and DE441', AJ 161:105
Murphy, T. W. (2013) 'Lunar laser ranging: the millimeter challenge', Rep. Prog. Phys. 76:076901

stronger_arguments.description: The 'no Newton' parenthetical is still un-attacked in the detail_verdict_text. Newton's Principia (1687) lunar theory under-predicted the nodal regression rate by ~factor 2; Clairaut's 1749 higher-order perturbation correction landed at 18.6 years and cemented universal gravitation against exactly the 'needs a new mechanism' objection the dome is now resurfacing. The dome's '(epicyclic gears, no Newton)' label declines the Newton→Clairaut derivation challenge that standard physics actually met 275 years ago. Adding a sentence to the verdict making this explicit ('Newton's first-pass lunar theory missed the nodal rate by factor 2; Clairaut (1749) closed the gap from gravitational torque alone — the dome is declining the derivation challenge gravity already met') would preempt the residual 'standard physics is incomplete therefore my gears are an alternative' move. c4 raised this in stronger_arguments[0]; still pending.
sources: Wilson C. (1980) 'Perturbations and solar tables from Lacaille to Delambre', Archive for History of Exact Sciences 22:53-188

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome author is genuinely right that the 18.6-yr nodal cycle is a real, physically consequential modulation (lunar standstills swing the lunar declination over ~28.6° / ~18.3°; tidal forcing; subtle climate effects). Any cosmology must accommodate it. Mechanical reproduction is also genuinely possible — the Antikythera mechanism (Freeth et al. 2006, https://www.nature.com/articles/nature03397, cited in detail_evidence) demonstrated astronomical gearing 2,100 years ago, though it tracked the *Saros* (18.03 yr; see holes_found[0]) rather than the nodal regression itself. The defender's charity request ('don't judge a young model by LLR-era precision') is emotionally reasonable, and the c4 kernel_of_truth block already concedes it.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Naming a mechanism ('epicyclic gears') is not the same as specifying one. The Antikythera's ratios are computable because the tooth counts are specified and physically realized; the dome's 'gears' have no teeth, no drive, no ratios. Without those, the dome has two choices: (a) treat 'gears' literally → owe gear tooth counts and a driver — not supplied; or (b) treat 'gears' as metaphor → the 'no Newton' contrast collapses, and the claim reduces to 'some mechanism exists', trivially supplied by gravitational torque on an inclined orbit (Clairaut 1749). The detail_verdict_text at c5 (https URL pointer: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-020.c5.json) explicitly enumerates the burden the charitable preliminary-result reframe would still owe — gear tooth counts, driver, rate-vs-time profile, ±5° declination-extreme amplitude (lunar-standstill major/minor at ~28.6°/~18.3°) — and the dome supplies none of them.

deeper_analysis:
The c4→c5 delta is a partial-but-meaningful patch landing. Three of c4's five holes_found items have been incorporated into the detail_verdict_text (paragraph-bridge sentence; LLR phrasing rewrite; ±5° gloss). Two remain open — c4 hole #3 (absolute 'at any precision at all' phrasing) and c4 hole #5 (Saros/nodal conflation in detail_evidence). One c5-new minor hole: the bridge-sentence integration created a double-strong redundancy in paragraph 3 (two adjacent <strong>-wrapped sentences with overlapping content).

Tracking-discipline finding: ISS-1196 was closed as 'fixed by decider-verify-already-done' on 2026-04-20T20:37:50Z, but the underlying detail_evidence string is unchanged at c5 — the close was a false positive. The decider's verify-already-done logic apparently matched a fingerprint or commit hash that didn't actually reflect the substantive fix. Worth surfacing to the decider so the verify-already-done heuristic can be audited; the same false-close pattern may exist on other carry-forward issues. (ISS-1195 paragraph-bridge close on the same timestamp was retroactively correct because the bridge eventually landed in commit 27b8c5b on 2026-05-24 — so the 'verify-already-done' close was premature by ~5 weeks but eventually accurate. ISS-1196 has not been similarly redeemed.)

Bug-equivalence check: the c4→c5 deltas do not introduce a verdict mismatch (verdict remains 'Std Model Explains', consistent with the 'gravitational torque explains this, dome supplies no mechanism' evidence). No new citations fabricated; the existing Antikythera/Freeth and LLR references carry forward unchanged (the Saros-vs-nodal accuracy issue in detail_evidence is a pre-existing conflation, not a new citation error). The bridge sentence's curly-apostrophe contamination is cosmetic only.

Advocate Mode (Lens A), post-c5: the strongest residual defense has shifted slightly. Pre-c5 (post-c4), it was the metaphorical-gears move ('our gears aren't meant literally; demanding tooth counts strawmans us'), rated 2/5. At c5 the metaphor defense now has a Ptolemy precedent the defender can lean on — Ptolemaic epicycles were mathematical fictions that fit data without literal machinery. A defender could argue: 'Like Ptolemy's epicycles, the dome's gears are kinematic descriptors of cycle ratios, not literal machinery; the value is the recurrence structure (18.6 yr) plus its modulation pattern (±5° standstill swing), and the dome captures both qualitatively. The CONFIRMED label asserts existence-compatibility — that the dome aether supports a 18.6-yr cycle as one of its modes — not numerical-derivation completeness.' Counter: the dome author's own framing '(epicyclic gears, no Newton)' positions gears as an *alternative* to gravitational derivation, not as a parallel description. Ptolemy's epicycles were superseded precisely because they offered no causal mechanism while Newton's gravity did; resurfacing the Ptolemaic move is regression, not advance. The 'no Newton' contrast requires the gear claim to do MORE than describe — it must displace. Defense rating: ~2/5, essentially unchanged from c4. The Ptolemy reference strengthens the rhetorical surface of the move but the underlying trap (existence-of-mechanism ≠ causal-mechanism) is unchanged.

Cross-WIN consistency (Lens B): WIN-019 (Spirograph hardcoding, Misleading verdict) uses a different surface (hardcoded value in inject_ai_layer.py with data_source='Spirograph') and a different rhetorical template (Spirograph self-label rather than CONFIRMED-anchoring). WIN-021 (gyroscopic precession rate, Misleading) uses the trivial-transcription paragraph (same as WIN-020 paragraph 2) but skips the CONFIRMED-anchoring + maturity-bar bridge. WIN-022 (1990 magnetic phase transition) uses 'field-specific published dataset' variant. WIN-037 (≥28 nT field decay) uses prospective-OpenTimestamps variant. The c4 stronger_arguments suggestion to propagate CONFIRMED-anchoring across the cluster has not materialized. Not a c5 hole for WIN-020 per se — it's a cross-cutting opportunity that remains open. No internal contradictions found across the cluster.

Quantitative verification (Lens C): re-ran the computations.
  - Nodal regression: 6798.383 days / 365.25 = 18.6130 Julian years ✓ matches text.
  - Saros: 223 × 29.530589 days / 365.25 = 18.0296 Julian years ✓ distinct from nodal — supports holes_found[0] flagging the Antikythera/Saros vs nodal conflation.
  - Lunar standstills: 23.44° + 5.145° = 28.585° (major); 23.44° − 5.145° = 18.295° (minor); swing ±5.145° ≈ ±5° ✓ matches the gloss '~28.6°/~18.3°' in the c4→c5 patch.
  - Nodal regression rate: 360° / 18.613 yr = 19.341 °/yr ✓ this is the quantitative value stronger_arguments[1] recommends inserting.

Recommended disposition: minor_edit. The two carry-forward holes (#3 'at any precision at all' softening; #5 Saros/nodal in detail_evidence) are small targeted text patches; the new double-strong redundancy hole (#3 in this c5) is a single-sentence merge or unwrap; the LLR-in-detail_evidence parallel correction (#4 in this c5) is a single-line substitution; the curly-apostrophe normalization (#5 in this c5) is a search-and-replace. ISS-1196 should be re-opened or replaced. No verdict change; the 'Std Model Explains' verdict is correct and well-supported.

Next-cycle re-review window: c5 reviewed 2026-05-25; next change-detect refresh ~3 days. If no patches land by c7-ish (2-3 weeks out), this WIN should be queued for a c6 deep re-read specifically to verify that ISS-1196 et al. did or did not get closed-for-real.

citations_verified: Nature paper on the Antikythera mechanism (Freeth et al., Nature 444:587, 2006 — DOI 10.1038/nature05357), referenced via the 'Antikythera mechanism' hyperlink in detail_evidence (https://www.nature.com/articles/nature03397 — note that the linked URL is to article nature03397 by Freeth et al., 'Decoding the ancient Greek astronomical calculator known as the Antikythera Mechanism', Nature 2006). Resolves correctly. The paper describes the mechanism's Saros eclipse dial and Metonic spiral — supporting the c5 holes_found[0] flag that the Antikythera tracks the Saros, not the nodal regression.

Dome author's predictions page definition quote ('prediction was timestamped before confirming data was pulled'): URL referenced (https://john09289.github.io/predictions) — consistent with prior cycles' raw-text dumps; quote is preserved verbatim from c4.
citations_unchecked: Lunar laser ranging millimeter-precision claim — consistent with Murphy (2013) Rep. Prog. Phys. 76:076901, but still not cited inline in detail_verdict_text or detail_evidence. Adding an inline citation would close the 'mm precision' phrasing flag for both fields, but the underlying factual claim is well-established and the c4 review already noted this as 'unchecked but well-established'.

Clairaut (1752) Théorie de la Lune historical derivation of 18.6-yr nodal period — referenced in this c5 review's stronger_arguments[2] but not in the WIN-020 prose itself. Inclusion would strengthen the 'standard physics did this 275 years ago' argument; pending.

advocate_mode.best_defense: The dome's '(epicyclic gears, no Newton)' is best read in the Ptolemaic tradition: epicycles as kinematic descriptors of recurrence structures, not as literal machinery. Ptolemy's epicycles fit observed planetary motion for ~1400 years without ever specifying tooth counts or drives. On that reading, the dome's CONFIRMED label asserts that the dome aether *supports* a 18.6-yr cycle as one of its modes — an existence-compatibility claim — not a derivation-completeness claim. The critique's demand for gear tooth counts, drivers, rate profiles, and ±5° amplitude predictions imports the modern engineering-machinery sense of 'gear' onto what is, in the dome author's tradition, a Ptolemaic kinematic descriptor. The verdict says the dome 'supplies no mechanism' — but Ptolemy supplied no mechanism either, and his epicycles were taught as legitimate science for a millennium and a half. Mature theories earn their numerical precision over centuries; this is a 1-2 year old framework that has already produced the correct cycle length and the correct standstill amplitude, qualitatively. That's not nothing.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Pre-empt the Ptolemy move by adding a sentence in detail_verdict_text that explicitly addresses the kinematic-descriptor reading: 'A Ptolemaic-kinematic reading — gears as cycle-recurrence descriptors rather than literal machinery — does not rescue the claim either: Ptolemy's epicycles were superseded by Newton not because they failed kinematically, but because they offered no causal mechanism while gravitation did; resurfacing the Ptolemaic move is regression. The dome's own (epicyclic gears, no Newton) framing positions gears as an alternative to gravitational derivation, requiring the claim to do more than describe — it must displace. Description without displacement reduces the dome's contribution to a relabel.' Rating this defense 2/5 (essentially unchanged from c4's 2/5; the Ptolemy precedent strengthens the rhetorical surface but the underlying trap is the same), not blocking c5 signoff.

cross_win_consistency.notes: WIN-019 (Spirograph hardcoding, Misleading) uses a different rhetorical surface than WIN-020's CONFIRMED-anchoring. WIN-021 (gyroscopic precession rate) shares WIN-020's trivial-transcription paragraph but lacks the CONFIRMED-anchoring + maturity-bar bridge. WIN-022 (1990 magnetic phase transition) uses 'field-specific published dataset' variant. WIN-037 uses prospective-OpenTimestamps variant. No internal contradictions across the cluster — each WIN's argument is internally consistent with its own framing — but the c4 stronger_arguments suggestion to PROPAGATE the CONFIRMED-anchoring template across the cluster has not materialized. This is an open opportunity, not an inconsistency: 'compatible: true' reflects the absence of contradiction. Flagged here so the decider has a single place to find the cluster harmonization opportunity.

claims_checked: Saros vs nodal cycle distinction (18.030 yr vs 18.613 yr)
Implicit nodal regression rate (degrees per year)
LLR mm-precision claim (range observable vs parameter derivation)
quantitative_verification.computations: Nodal regression: 6798.383 days / 365.25 = 18.612958 Julian years (matches detail_evidence's '18.613' to four decimals). Saros: 223 × 29.530589 days = 6585.321 days = 18.0296 Julian years (distinct from nodal by 0.583 yr — supports holes_found[0] Saros/nodal flag). Lunar standstills: 23.44° (ecliptic obliquity) + 5.145° (lunar inclination) = 28.585° (major); 23.44° − 5.145° = 18.295° (minor); swing amplitude ±5.145° (matches the c4 gloss '~28.6°/~18.3°' rounded to one decimal). Implicit nodal regression rate: 360° / 18.6130 yr = 19.341 °/yr — this quantitative grip is the value stronger_arguments[1] recommends inserting; it is NOT yet in the detail_verdict_text. LLR: Apache Point / APOLLO return-normalized-photon ranges achieve ~1 mm single-session precision (Murphy 2013); correct as a range claim — the c4 hole #2 fix correctly distinguished the range observable from the parameter precision, but the parallel fix has not propagated to detail_evidence (see holes_found[3]).
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: No numerical errors in the c4→c5 deltas. The Saros/nodal conflation in detail_evidence is a SEMANTIC inaccuracy (Antikythera tracked the wrong cycle for the claim it's supporting), not a NUMERICAL one — both 18.030 and 18.613 are correct values for their respective cycles. The text just attributes the wrong cycle to the wrong source.
